title: Type Conversion
subtitle: Converting between data types
goal: Learn how to convert values between different data types.
explanation: JavaScript lets you change a value's type.
The functions Number(), String(), and Boolean() are used for this.
They create a new value of a different type.
Type conversion is common when working with forms, APIs, and user input.
Understanding type conversion helps you avoid unexpected program behavior.

analogy.title: Analogy
analogy.text: Imagine water. You can pour it from a glass into a bottle or a cup. The content stays the same, but the container changes. JavaScript can change a value's type the same way.

remember: A value's type can be changed.

mistake.title: Common mistake
mistake.text: Not every string can be converted to a number. For example, Number("Hello") returns NaN.

quiz.question: Which function converts the string "42" to a number?
